message:
fix: move useRef before early return (hooks violation), raise rate limits (429 flood)

- MissionControlDialog: phaseAdvancingRef useRef/useEffect moved above
  the 'if (!open) return null' early return to satisfy React Rules of
  Hooks. This caused 'Rendered more hooks than during the previous
  render' crash when opening Mission Control.

- Rate limits: apiLimiter raised from 200 to 600 req/min, publicLimiter
  from 60 to 120 req/min. Dashboard pages fire 15-20 SSE streams + REST
  calls per switch; 200 req/min caused 429 floods on normal usage.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -161,6 +161,13 @@ export function MissionControlDialog({ open, onClose }: MissionControlDialogProp
     }
   }, [open])
 
+  // #6828 — Ref-based guard prevents double-click from skipping a phase.
+  // The guard resets after one React render cycle via useEffect.
+  // MUST be before the early return — React Rules of Hooks require
+  // hooks to run in the same order on every render.
+  const phaseAdvancingRef = useRef(false)
+  useEffect(() => { phaseAdvancingRef.current = false }, [state.phase])
+
   if (!open) return null
 
   const isLaunching = state.phase === 'launching'
@@ -177,11 +184,6 @@ export function MissionControlDialog({ open, onClose }: MissionControlDialogProp
   const canGoBack =
     state.phase === 'assign' || state.phase === 'blueprint'
 
-  // #6828 — Ref-based guard prevents double-click from skipping a phase.
-  // The guard resets after one React render cycle via useEffect.
-  const phaseAdvancingRef = useRef(false)
-  useEffect(() => { phaseAdvancingRef.current = false }, [state.phase])
-
   const handleNext = () => {
     if (phaseAdvancingRef.current) return
     phaseAdvancingRef.current = true
